jokes.text: A disappointingly good idea from Jerry.
jokes.setup: Jerry presents a thoughtful time capsule idea with his mother's diaries
jokes.punchline: Leslie's backhanded compliment reveals her low expectations for Jerry
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Leslie's relationship with Jerry - she can't even give him a pure compliment. Economy and character integration are strong.

jokes.text: Jerrold starred in his school production of Peter Pan. He was a beautiful Tinker Bell.
jokes.setup: Leslie reads from Jerry's mother's diary expecting mundane town history
jokes.punchline: Reveals embarrassing detail about Jerry playing a female role
jokes.explanation: Standard embarrassing Jerry moment. The 'beautiful' detail adds a nice touch but it's fairly predictable cringe.

jokes.text: It was an all-boys school.
jokes.setup: Jerry's feminine role in Peter Pan is revealed
jokes.punchline: Jerry's matter-of-fact explanation makes it even more awkward
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation that makes the situation worse. Perfect economy - five words that land a solid punch. Jerry's obliviousness is perfect.

jokes.text: Why don't we put Eduardo in there and seal the top so that he suffocates and dies?
jokes.setup: Time capsule discussion continues normally
jokes.punchline: Tom casually suggests murdering Eduardo over jealousy
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that fits Tom's petty jealousy. The casual delivery makes it funnier but it's a bit obvious given his situation.

jokes.text: Picture of my ex-girlfriend Lucy with a mustache drawn on her face and stink lines coming off her, 'cause she stinks.
jokes.setup: Tom presents his time capsule item
jokes.punchline: Reveals petty, childish revenge drawing
jokes.explanation: Pure Tom pettiness. The visual of adult Tom drawing mustaches is funny but fairly juvenile humor.

jokes.text: Luckily, when you're the guy, you can just tell people she's crazy. That's what they always do on Entourage.
jokes.setup: Tom explains his breakup strategy
jokes.punchline: Reveals he gets relationship advice from Entourage
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom character moment - getting life advice from a shallow TV show. The Entourage reference dates it but fits his character perfectly.

jokes.text: The four horse meals of the egg-pork-alypse
jokes.setup: Ron describes a breakfast dish from JJ's diner
jokes.punchline: Portmanteau combining 'apocalypse' with pork and eggs
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that perfectly captures Ron's love of meat and biblical references. Creative portmanteau that's both funny and quotable.

jokes.text: Not like you get extra credit for this, but I did type it from memory.
jokes.setup: Leslie presents her comprehensive Pawnee history
jokes.punchline: Claims she doesn't want credit while obviously fishing for praise
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie Knope moment - false modesty while clearly wanting recognition for her overachievement. Character integration is excellent.

jokes.text: Pawnee: Welcome, German soldiers.
jokes.setup: Leslie reads historical Pawnee slogans
jokes.punchline: Town welcomed Nazi soldiers during WWII
jokes.explanation: Dark historical humor that establishes Pawnee's incompetent government. The casual delivery of this horrible decision is perfectly Parks and Rec.

jokes.text: After the Nazis took France, our mayor kind of panicked.
jokes.setup: The welcome German soldiers slogan
jokes.punchline: Massive understatement about the mayor's Nazi-welcoming response
jokes.explanation: Brilliant understatement. 'Kind of panicked' is perfect deadpan delivery for such an extreme historical overreaction.

jokes.text: Pawnee: Welcome, Vietnamese soldiers.
jokes.setup: Pattern of welcoming enemy soldiers established
jokes.punchline: Pawnee repeated the same mistake in Vietnam
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Pawnee learned nothing from WWII. Less impact than the first one but solid running gag.

jokes.text: Pawnee: Engage with Zorp.
jokes.setup: Historical slogans continue
jokes.punchline: Town was taken over by cult with alien deity
jokes.explanation: Introduces Zorp mythology that becomes important to the series. 'Engage with' is perfectly bureaucratic language for cult worship.

jokes.text: Pawnee: Zorp is dead. Long live Zorp.
jokes.setup: Zorp cult reference established
jokes.punchline: Contradictory slogan about dead/living Zorp
jokes.explanation: Parodies 'The King is dead, long live the King' while showing cult confusion. Good escalation of Zorp mythology.

jokes.text: Pawnee: Birthplace of Julia Roberts. That was a lie, she sued.
jokes.setup: Historical slogans showing poor decisions
jokes.punchline: Town lied about celebrity birthplace and got sued
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Pawnee's desperation for relevance. The matter-of-fact delivery of the lawsuit makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Pawnee: Home of the world-famous Julia Roberts lawsuit.
jokes.setup: Julia Roberts lie led to lawsuit
jokes.punchline: Town proudly claims the lawsuit itself as achievement
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - turning their shame into pride. Shows Pawnee's ability to spin any disaster as a positive. Great structure and payoff.

jokes.text: Pawnee: Welcome, Taliban soldiers.
jokes.setup: Pattern of welcoming enemy soldiers
jokes.punchline: Pawnee never learned, now welcoming terrorists
jokes.explanation: Completes the trilogy of welcoming enemies. Less impact than earlier ones but shows consistency of Pawnee's poor judgment.

jokes.text: Pawnee: First in friendship, fourth in obesity.
jokes.setup: Current Pawnee slogan after all the disasters
jokes.punchline: Pairs positive trait with embarrassing health ranking
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending to the slogan sequence. The juxtaposition of pride and shame in one slogan is quintessential Parks and Rec.

jokes.text: It's time to encapsulate the future.
jokes.setup: Journalist suggests headline for time capsule story
jokes.punchline: Awkward forced pun mixing 'encapsulate' and time capsule
jokes.explanation: Weak pun that feels forced. Shows Shauna's journalistic mediocrity but the wordplay itself isn't clever enough.

jokes.text: The parks department cuts the crapsule, buries the time capsule.
jokes.setup: First headline pun
jokes.punchline: Even worse pun with 'crapsule'
jokes.explanation: Terrible pun that's funny because it's so bad. 'Crapsule' is aggressively unfunny, which makes it work as character comedy for Shauna.

jokes.text: I thought you would be younger... And a girl.
jokes.setup: Leslie meets Kelly, the Twilight enthusiast
jokes.punchline: Expected teenage girl but got adult man
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of expectations about Twilight fans. Leslie's blunt honesty is in character and the pause makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Present company excluded. Oh, thank you. Uh, I was talking about me.
jokes.setup: Andy calls Eduardo the handsomest dude he's ever seen, then says present company excluded
jokes.punchline: Andy clarifies he meant himself, not Chris
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy moment - his narcissistic obliviousness is endearing rather than awful. Good misdirection with the 'present company excluded' setup.

jokes.text: I can literally see my face in my shoes.
jokes.setup: Chris praises Andy's shoeshine work
jokes.punchline: Hyperbolic enthusiasm about reflection in shoes
jokes.explanation: Classic Chris over-enthusiasm. The 'literally' is perfectly in character but the joke itself is fairly basic.

jokes.text: He didn't... he didn't pay me.
jokes.setup: Chris enthusiastically praises the shoeshine and leaves
jokes.punchline: Andy realizes he worked for free
jokes.explanation: Good deflation after Chris's enthusiasm. Andy's confused delivery sells his realization that he's been had.

jokes.text: I cannot figure out who my boss is.
jokes.setup: Andy thinks Chris is his boss but Ann says he's not
jokes.punchline: Andy has no idea about basic workplace hierarchy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy confusion about adult responsibilities. His genuine frustration with basic workplace concepts is endearing and funny.

jokes.text: Somehow that fish ended up dead in a cowboy boot.
jokes.setup: Ann explains why Andy will screw up her relationship with Chris
jokes.punchline: Bizarre, unexplained fish death scenario
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd detail that perfectly captures Andy's ability to create chaos from simple situations. The specificity makes it memorable.

jokes.text: The town of Forks, Washington is exactly like Pawnee, except for the climate and the vampires.
jokes.setup: Kelly argues Twilight is relevant to Pawnee
jokes.punchline: Lists the two most fundamental differences as exceptions
jokes.explanation: Good logic joke - the 'except for' completely undermines his argument. Kelly's obliviousness to his own contradiction works well.

jokes.text: He brought handcuffs with him. This whole thing was planned.
jokes.setup: Kelly handcuffs himself to protest
jokes.punchline: Leslie realizes the premeditation
jokes.explanation: Straightforward escalation revealing Kelly's calculation. Leslie's detective work is in character but the joke is fairly basic.

jokes.text: Cool people make the rules. They don't break the rules.
jokes.setup: Leslie responds to someone calling the situation cool
jokes.punchline: Earnest definition of coolness focused on rule-following
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie moment - she genuinely believes rule-following is cool. Character integration is flawless, showing her earnest bureaucratic worldview.

jokes.text: Damn it. Again?
jokes.setup: Leslie explains the Twilight handcuffing situation
jokes.punchline: Ron's weary response implies this happens regularly
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy and character work. Two words that perfectly establish this as a recurring problem. Ron's exhausted familiarity is perfect.

jokes.text: What look did your mom have on her face when she realized her son was a complete failure?
jokes.setup: Ron needles Kelly about looking sad over a girl
jokes.punchline: Kelly fires back with brutal mom insult
jokes.explanation: Standard escalation of insults. Shows Kelly has some fight in him but the comeback itself is fairly generic.

jokes.text: And if I had an hour alone with Robert Pattinson, he would forget all about Skinnylegs McGee.
jokes.setup: Donna lists types of books she likes
jokes.punchline: Confident claim she could steal Robert Pattinson from Kristen Stewart
jokes.explanation: Pure Donna confidence and sass. 'Skinnylegs McGee' is a perfect Donna nickname. Character integration is excellent.

jokes.text: This muscle-headed storm trooper doesn't scare me.
jokes.setup: Security guard threatens to remove Kelly
jokes.punchline: Kelly calls guard muscle-headed while clearly terrified
jokes.explanation: Basic false bravado setup. The immediate reversal is predictable but serves the character moment adequately.

jokes.text: No! No! No! Please! I am very scared of you!
jokes.setup: Kelly claims he's not scared
jokes.punchline: Immediately breaks and admits terror
jokes.explanation: Predictable coward reversal but the panicked delivery sells it. Standard physical comedy escalation.

jokes.text: He brought a pillow.
jokes.setup: Leslie expects Kelly to give up from hunger and discomfort
jokes.punchline: Kelly came prepared for extended stay
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Kelly's preparation. Visual gag works well and the economy is excellent - three words deflate Leslie's plan.

jokes.text: Hey, Leslie, I'm making some sleepytime tea. You want any?
jokes.setup: Kelly is supposedly protesting
jokes.punchline: He's made himself comfortable and offering hospitality
jokes.explanation: Good role reversal - the protester becomes the host. Shows how Kelly has turned the situation to his advantage.

jokes.text: Twilight is dope. I told you. I couldn't put it down. It was like she was peering into my soul.
jokes.setup: Tom was forced to read Twilight overnight
jokes.punchline: Tom is completely converted and speaking in clichés
jokes.explanation: Shows Tom's susceptibility to trends and peer pressure. The 'peering into my soul' is perfect overwrought fan language.

jokes.text: There's a second book? And a third and a fourth. No [bleep] way.
jokes.setup: Tom is already hooked on first book
jokes.punchline: Discovers there's an entire series to consume
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Tom's newfound obsession. His excitement about more content to consume fits his character.

jokes.text: Those are sports sandals. They're for adventure racing. The human foot is the ultimate technology.
jokes.setup: Andy doesn't know what Chris's footwear is
jokes.punchline: Chris's overwrought explanation of sandal technology
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chris moment - turning simple sandals into profound technology. 'Ultimate technology' is classic Chris hyperbole about fitness.

jokes.text: Dude, that is the coolest sentence I've ever heard somebody talk.
jokes.setup: Chris explains foot technology
jokes.punchline: Andy's grammatically confused praise
jokes.explanation: 'Heard somebody talk' instead of 'heard someone say' is perfect Andy verbal confusion. Shows his genuine admiration for Chris's fitness speak.

jokes.text: Hey, you know who should wear these shoes? Monkeys.
jokes.setup: Discussion about foot technology and shoes
jokes.punchline: Andy's random monkey shoe observation
jokes.explanation: Random Andy observation that somehow makes sense - monkeys do have human-like feet. His non-sequitur logic is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: I'm nice. Good! Nice. What else? I'm in a band. Band! What else? That's it.
jokes.setup: Chris asks Andy to list his attractive qualities
jokes.punchline: Andy only has two basic qualities and runs out immediately
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy self-awareness - he knows he's simple but doesn't try to oversell himself. Chris's enthusiastic responses make it funnier.

jokes.text: Am I Team Edward? Yes. Do I share his concerns about turning Bella, though? Absolutely not.
jokes.setup: Tom has become obsessed with Twilight
jokes.punchline: He's diving deep into vampire romance philosophy
jokes.explanation: Shows Tom's complete conversion to fandom. The serious analysis of vampire romance fits his tendency to overcommit to things.

jokes.text: Sometimes I think she's in the volturi.
jokes.setup: Leslie seems tense and frustrated
jokes.punchline: Tom compares her to Twilight villains
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Tom's new obsession - he's now viewing real life through Twilight lens. Shows how completely he's been converted.

jokes.text: Well, for that guy, life in Pawnee is him and his daughter reading that book.
jokes.setup: Leslie explains why Twilight should be included despite protests
jokes.punchline: Reframes the situation as genuinely representative of Pawnee life
jokes.explanation: Shows Leslie's ability to find genuine meaning in seemingly trivial situations. Her empathy and practical wisdom are perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Crackpot convention.
jokes.setup: Ron warns that giving in will encourage more protests
jokes.punchline: Leslie decides to embrace the chaos with a public forum
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie solution - turn a problem into an opportunity for civic engagement. 'Crackpot convention' is memorable phrasing.

jokes.text: I think we should put in the Bible. Great.
jokes.setup: Leslie asks for suggestions, specifically mentions no religious items
jokes.punchline: First suggestion is immediately the Bible, Leslie responds sarcastically
jokes.explanation: Predictable but effective. Shows how citizens ignore rules immediately. Leslie's deadpan 'Great' is perfect bureaucratic frustration.

jokes.text: David Matthews! Crash into me! Yes! I love that song.
jokes.setup: Eduardo mentions liking Dave Matthews
jokes.punchline: Andy gets excited about shared musical taste
jokes.explanation: Basic bonding moment over mainstream music. Shows Andy's earnestness but the joke itself is fairly weak.

jokes.text: That means 'thank you'? Yes. Nailed it.
jokes.setup: Eduardo says 'gracias'
jokes.punchline: Andy proudly celebrates basic Spanish comprehension
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy moment - celebrating minimal achievement with maximum enthusiasm. His pride in basic Spanish is endearingly childish.

jokes.text: There really is a tremendous amount of quivering.
jokes.setup: Marcia complains about Twilight's sexual content
jokes.punchline: Focuses obsessively on 'quivering' detail
jokes.explanation: Shows how censorious minds focus on exactly what they claim to oppose. 'Tremendous amount of quivering' is a great phrase.

jokes.text: So too Christian and not Christian enough?
jokes.setup: Two groups complain Twilight is both too Christian and not Christian enough
jokes.punchline: Leslie points out the logical contradiction
jokes.explanation: Good observation about contradictory complaints. Leslie's ability to spot the absurdity is in character and the setup pays off well.

jokes.text: Do you see the irony here? No? Okay.
jokes.setup: Leslie points out contradiction about Twilight
jokes.punchline: Realizes no one understands irony, gives up
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie moment of trying to educate the public and failing. The quick abandonment when no one gets it is both funny and realistic.

jokes.text: Go back to Russia, commie! Hey, you go back to Russia! And you go back to wherever you came from!
jokes.setup: Public forum descends into chaos
jokes.punchline: Everyone tells everyone else to go to Russia regardless of politics
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of political confusion. Shows how quickly civil discourse breaks down but could be tighter.

jokes.text: Why would I go to Russia? I have no interest in traveling to Russia.
jokes.setup: Someone tells citizen to go to Russia
jokes.punchline: Citizen takes insult literally as travel suggestion
jokes.explanation: Good literal interpretation of political insult. Shows citizen's oblivious nature but could be stronger.

jokes.text: I just don't get why you broke up with me. Is it an Edward-Bella-Jacob type situation?
jokes.setup: Andy confronts April about their breakup
jokes.punchline: Uses Twilight love triangle to understand his situation
jokes.explanation: Shows how Twilight has infected even Andy's thinking. Using vampire romance to understand real relationships is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: It's nothing to do with Twilight.
jokes.setup: Andy's elaborate Twilight metaphor for their breakup
jokes.punchline: April's flat dismissal of the entire comparison
jokes.explanation: Perfect April deadpan deflation of Andy's romantic metaphor. Six words that completely shut down his elaborate comparison.

jokes.text: Imagine if your boss was Angelina Jolie, and then one day, she just started dating your ex-boyfriend.
jokes.setup: Tom explains why Ron dating Wendy bothers him
jokes.punchline: Uses celebrity comparison to explain workplace awkwardness
jokes.explanation: Good analogy that makes Tom's position understandable. Shows his ability to find celebrity comparisons for everything.

jokes.text: Well, not if I had great new boyfriend, dumbass.
jokes.setup: Tom's elaborate justification for his jealousy
jokes.punchline: Lucy cuts through it with simple logic and insult
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of Tom's self-pity. 'Dumbass' is perfectly placed and her logic is unassailable.

jokes.text: You're small enough for me to throw you around.
jokes.setup: Lucy lists Tom's attractive qualities
jokes.punchline: Includes his small size as a sexual advantage
jokes.explanation: Unexpected sexual confidence that turns Tom's size 'disadvantage' into an asset. Good character work for both Lucy and Tom's dynamic.

jokes.text: Except for Turnip.
jokes.setup: Leslie firmly states no ashes will go in the time capsule
jokes.punchline: Immediately makes exception for the cat
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic timing and contradiction. Two words that completely undermine her firm stance. Economy is excellent.

jokes.text: I got two microphones, a four-track recorder, and every bass tab to Dave Matthews ever. Vamos!
jokes.setup: Eduardo is leaving but Andy wants to jam
jokes.punchline: Eduardo came prepared for extensive Dave Matthews session
jokes.explanation: Shows Eduardo's commitment to the musical connection. The extensive preparation for one jam session is endearingly over-prepared.

jokes.text: Usually people don't care about anything. These people are weirdos, but they're weirdos who care.
jokes.setup: Ben reflects on Pawnee citizens fighting over time capsule
jokes.punchline: Finds the positive in civic engagement despite the chaos
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ben wisdom that captures Parks and Rec's heart. Finds genuine meaning in civic chaos. Great character integration and message.

jokes.text: And sorry about the weird blank gap in the middle. A man named Jerry Gergich screwed up the recording somehow. He had one job to do.
jokes.setup: Leslie records time capsule message
jokes.punchline: Preemptively blames Jerry for future technical failures
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke that blames Jerry for a problem that hasn't happened yet but inevitably will. Perfect character assassination.

jokes.text: He was a really good bassist. I was actually started to like him. I know. That's what made me start to hate him.
jokes.setup: Andy admits Eduardo was talented and likable
jokes.punchline: April explains this made Eduardo more threatening
jokes.explanation: Good insight into jealousy psychology. April's honesty about how likability made Eduardo worse is perfectly her character.

jokes.text: The nice and band totally worked.
jokes.setup: Andy followed Chris's advice about being nice and promoting his band
jokes.punchline: April acknowledges the strategy succeeded
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Andy's simple self-assessment. Shows that basic authenticity won April back.

jokes.text: And also, there's a pretzel stand over there that literally serves the best pretzel I've ever had.
jokes.setup: Chris compliments Ann based on Andy's recommendation
jokes.punchline: Immediately transitions to pretzel enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chris moment - equal enthusiasm for romance and snacks. His 'literally' the best pretzel is classic Chris hyperbole.

jokes.text: Dude, what did I just say? How about you shut up?
jokes.setup: Kelly asks for quiet during Twilight movie, people keep talking
jokes.punchline: Kelly becomes aggressively protective of his movie experience
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Kelly's possessiveness over the Twilight experience. Shows how his obsession has grown more militant.
